# SimDrive

SimDrive is an MCP-native iOS simulator and real-device automation tool. Your MCP client
(Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or any MCP-compatible agent) drives the simulator via 32
vision-first tools ‚Äî record once, then replay deterministically in CI with zero AI cost.
It ships as a Python package (`simdrive`) and an MCP server command (`simdrive`). The key
insight: use AI to record, use a deterministic engine to replay. Your client pays for tokens
during recording; every subsequent CI run is free. SimDrive itself does **not** require an
API key.

> Repo note: the GitHub repository is currently `SyncTek-LLC/specterqa-ios`; rename to
> `SyncTek-LLC/simdrive` is pending. The PyPI package name is **`simdrive`**.

## Install

```bash
# CLI + MCP server (single install)
pip install simdrive
```

Requires: macOS, Xcode 15+, Python 3.10+

## Use via MCP (Claude Code / Claude Desktop)

Add to `.claude/mcp.json` (Claude Code) or `claude_desktop_config.json` (Claude Desktop):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "simdrive": {
      "command": "simdrive"
    }
  }
}
```

Then in your agent: _"Run a smoke test on the Example Reader app and record it as Example Reader-smoke."_
Your agent drives the simulator, records every action as a deterministic recording. Future
CI runs replay that recording without calling the agent.

## Minimum Viable Session (5 calls)

```python
session_start(bundle_id="com.example.app")
observe()                                  # observe initial screen
tap(label="Sign In")                       # interact
observe()                                  # verify state
record_stop(session_id="...", name="signin-smoke")  # save replay
session_end(session_id="...")              # clean up
```

## MCP Tool Registry (32 tools ‚Äî canonical)

The exhaustive, machine-readable list lives in `simdrive/src/simdrive/server.py::_TOOLS`.
The categorized human-readable surface lives in `docs/MCP_TOOL_SURFACE.md`. The 32 tools
exposed by the MCP server are:

- `session_start` ‚Äî Start a SimDrive session on a booted iOS Simulator (or opted-in physical device).
- `session_end` ‚Äî Stop the runner and clean up the session.
- `session_status` ‚Äî Report active sessions, modes, and version.
- `observe` ‚Äî Capture an annotated screenshot + element list (vision-first primitive).
- `tap` ‚Äî Tap by mark id, label, identifier, or raw coordinates.
- `swipe` ‚Äî Swipe in a direction or between two points.
- `type_text` ‚Äî Type into a focused text field.
- `press_key` ‚Äî Press a named keyboard key.
- `record_start` ‚Äî Begin a new recording (clears the step buffer).
- `record_stop` ‚Äî Save the current recording and clear the buffer.
- `replay` ‚Äî Run a saved recording end-to-end against a booted target.
- `list_devices` ‚Äî List booted iOS simulators (UDID, name, runtime, state).
- `logs` ‚Äî Get recent app console logs from the iOS Simulator.
- `perf` ‚Äî Real-time CPU / RSS / thread count for the app under test.
- `perf_baseline` ‚Äî Capture a metrics baseline.
- `perf_compare` ‚Äî Compare current metrics against the baseline.
- `memory` ‚Äî Detailed memory breakdown via the macOS `footprint` tool.
- `doctor` ‚Äî Environment readiness: Xcode, simulator runtimes, booted devices, runner build status.
- `app_state` ‚Äî App lifecycle state (foreground, background, suspended).
- `apps` ‚Äî List apps installed on a booted simulator.
- `crashes` ‚Äî App crashes since the session started.
- `dismiss_first_launch_alerts` ‚Äî Dismiss system permission alerts at first launch.
- `pre_grant_permissions` ‚Äî Pre-grant iOS app permissions before launch.
- `set_appearance` ‚Äî Toggle dark or light mode on the iOS Simulator.
- `dismiss_sheet` ‚Äî Dismiss a presented sheet by swiping down.
- `list_replays` ‚Äî List saved recordings with their names, step counts, and timestamps.
- `validate_replay` ‚Äî Parse and validate a saved recording without executing it.
- `version` ‚Äî Return the installed SimDrive version.
- `clear_field` ‚Äî Clear a text field.
- `load_journey` ‚Äî Load a recorded journey for replay.
- `lint_recordings` ‚Äî Lint saved recordings for state-contract drift.
- `migrate_recording` ‚Äî Migrate a recording from an older schema to the current one.

## Use via CLI

```bash
# First-time setup
simdrive --version           # verify install
simdrive                     # start the MCP server (stdio)
```

(Additional CLI subcommands are exposed via the underlying `simdrive` console script. See
`docs/MCP_TOOL_SURFACE.md` for the canonical tool surface and `README.md` for usage.)

## Pricing

See https://synctek.io/products/simdrive for current tiers. SimDrive itself does not require
an API key; record-time AI cost is borne by your MCP client (BYOK).

## Links

- Homepage: https://synctek.io/products/simdrive
- GitHub (docs + issues): https://github.com/SyncTek-LLC/specterqa-ios (rename to `simdrive` pending)
- Support: support@synctek.io
- Security: security@simdrive.dev (routes to maintainer until inbox live)
